assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements.

The intragroup dividend has already been declared and a receivable has been recognised, so the exposure is assumed to be a recognised intragroup monetary receivable rather than only a forecast transaction.
The receivable/payable is assumed to be denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure for at least one group entity.
No facts indicate that the intended hedge is a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation rather than a hedge of the dividend receivable itself.

approaches.label: Ordinary intragroup item not eligible in consolidation
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 generally allows only items with an external party to be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements. Intragroup transactions are excluded unless a specific foreign-currency exception applies.
approaches.practical_implication: If the dividend item does not fall within an explicit IFRS 9 exception, hedge accounting in consolidation is not available.

source_contributions.summary: Only exposures with a party external to the reporting entity are ordinarily eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of recognized intragroup monetary item
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 expressly permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item, such as a receivable/payable, to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable/payable is a recognized intragroup monetary item
The group entities involved have different functional currencies
The foreign currency gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and affect consolidated profit or loss
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are met at inception
approaches.practical_implication: This is the main potentially available route for a booked intragroup dividend receivable exposed to FX risk in consolidation.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 allows FX risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify in consolidated financial statements when consolidation does not fully eliminate the exposure.
Paragraph 6.4.1 requires formal designation, documentation and satisfaction of hedge effectiveness criteria.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 permits a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction to qualify only under specific FX conditions, but that route is for forecast transactions. A recognised dividend receivable is no longer merely forecast.
conditions: The transaction is still a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction
It is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it
Its foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss
approaches.practical_implication: If the dividend has already given rise to a receivable, analyze the hedge under the intragroup monetary-item exception instead.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraphs 6.3.6 and B6.3.5 allow certain highly probable forecast intragroup FX exposures to qualify in consolidated financial statements.
Paragraphs 6.3.3 and 6.5.2(b) require the forecast transaction to be highly probable and of a type that can affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.reasoning: IFRIC 16 addresses hedges of the foreign currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation, measured by net assets included in the consolidated financial statements. That is a different hedged item from a separate intragroup dividend receivable.
conditions: The hedged item is an amount of net assets of a foreign operation included in the consolidated financial statements
The designated risk is the foreign currency exposure between the foreign operation and a parent entity's functional currency
The hedge is designated only once in the ultimate consolidated financial statements
approaches.practical_implication: Do not use net investment hedge documentation unless the exposure being hedged is the foreign operation's net assets, not the dividend receivable.

source_contributions.summary: The Interpretation limits this hedge model to foreign currency risk on net investments in foreign operations included in consolidated financial statements.
The hedged item is limited to an amount of net assets and the same net investment exposure can qualify only once in the consolidated financial statements.
